<!-- AUTOGENERATED:START - Do not edit between these markers -->
# teams2 scaffold manifest

Create a Teams app manifest.json file

## Usage

```
teams2 scaffold manifest [options]
```

## Options

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `-p, --path <path>` | [OPTIONAL] Output directory (default: current directory) |
| `-n, --name <name>` | App name |
| `-d, --domain <domain>` | [OPTIONAL] Valid domain for the manifest |
| `-b, --bot-id <id>` | [OPTIONAL] Bot ID (uses placeholder if not provided) |
| `-i, --interactive` | [OPTIONAL] Force interactive mode even when args provided |
<!-- AUTOGENERATED:END -->

## Details

Generates a `manifest.json` file for a Teams app. Useful when you want to create a manifest locally before uploading or packaging.

### Interactive Mode

Without `--name`, the CLI prompts for:
- App name
- Bot scopes (personal, team, group chat)
- Short/long description
- Developer info customization

Force interactive mode even when arguments are provided with `--interactive`.

### Placeholder Bot ID

If `--bot-id` is not provided, the manifest uses `{{BOT_ID}}` as a placeholder. Replace it with your actual bot ID before uploading.

### Examples

Quick generation:

```bash
teams2 scaffold manifest --name "My Bot"
```

Full control:

```bash
teams2 scaffold manifest --name "My Bot" --bot-id <id> --domain mybot.azurewebsites.net --path ./app
```
